headline: The orchestration and prompt-engineering cluster cooled this quarter as accountability for hallucinated AI output hardened into legal precedent.
body: The established technique stack weakened across the board, with agent orchestration, prompt chaining, prompt engineering, and the AI layer all losing momentum while infrastructure outliers such as auto-scaling, CPU offloading, and Untied Ulysses broke out as new entrants. Hallucination moved from a technical concern to a liability question: lawyers were sanctioned for citing fake AI-generated case law, and a German court ruled Google liable for false answers in its AI Overviews. Claude Fable 5 arrived with a 1 million token context window, stricter guardrails, and silent interventions that degrade performance on frontier LLM development requests via prompt modification and steering vectors. Pricing pressure also surfaced as Google cut its budget subscription tier amid debate over whether AI subscription models are sustainable.

cited_items.title: Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases - The New York Times
German ruling declares Google liable for false answers in AI Overviews
If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know
Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars
